content_blocks.text:
18. Vimshottari Dasha and other Types of Dashas. Hundreds
of yogas are dealt with in chapters VI, VII and VIII and
such a detailed treatment is difficult to come across elsewhere,
Each chapter is comprehensive book by itself and it is for the
reader to verify for himself the contents.

Not much is known about the author except that he was
the son of a great scholar Venkatadri and belonged to the
Bharadwaja Gotra as he himself says =

श्री विद्याधिक बेङ्खयव्रितनयः श्री वैद्यनाथसुधीः ।
भारद्वाज कुलोद्भवस्य विवुवः श्री वैडूयद्वरिह ।
ज्योतिश्शास्त्रविशारदस्य तनयः श्रीवद्यमाथः सुधीः ।।

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
श्री विद्याधिक बेङ्खयव्रितनयः श्री वैद्यनाथसुधीः ।
भारद्वाज कुलोद्भवस्य विवुवः श्री वैडूयद्वरिह ।
ज्योतिश्शास्त्रविशारदस्य तनयः श्रीवद्यमाथः सुधीः ।।

content_blocks.text:
from the abundancy of quotations found from the work
'Sarvartha Chintamani,' some opine that father of Vaidyanatha
Dikshita is none other than Venkatesa the author of 'Sarvartha
Chintamani'. But, this deserves further investigation and the
suggestion appears to be incondusive. The suggestion that the
author was indentical with the writer of the celebrated work
'Prataparadriya' on poestics, is totally incorrect, for the latter
was Vidyānāthā (विद्यानाथ), the confusion being due to the
unfortunate similarity in spelling. The appellation 'Dikshita'
clearly denotes that he must have performed some biz sacrifices
of the type 'Somayaga' etc.

verse_data.translation:
from the abundancy of quotations found from the work
'Sarvartha Chintamani,' some opine that father of Vaidyanatha
Dikshita is none other than Venkatesa the author of 'Sarvartha
Chintamani'. But, this deserves further investigation and the
suggestion appears to be incondusive. The suggestion that the
author was indentical with the writer of the celebrated work
'Prataparadriya' on poestics, is totally incorrect, for the latter
was Vidyānāthā (विद्यानाथ), the confusion being due to the
unfortunate similarity in spelling. The appellation 'Dikshita'
clearly denotes that he must have performed some biz sacrifices
of the type 'Somayaga' etc.
verse_data.topic: Discussion on author's identity and potential confusion

content_blocks.text:
The author belonged to South India and most probably
to Karnataka or Andhrapradesh.

Prof. Katva has stated that he belonged to the 14th cen-
tury and lived in Mysore. As far as we could gather, the
family tree is as follows.--

If any further details are known to he readers, they are
requested to send them to us so that the same can be added for
throwing more light about the author of this great work.

raw_text:
(5)
18. Vimshottari Dasha and other Types of Dashas. Hundreds
of yogas are dealt with in chapters VI, VII and VIII and
such a detailed treatment is difficult to come across elsewhere,
Each chapter is comprehensive book by itself and it is for the
reader to verify for himself the contents.
THE AUTHOR
Not much is known about the author except that he was
the son of a great scholar Venkatadri and belonged to the
Bharadwaja Gotra as he himself says =
श्री विद्याधिक बेङ्खयव्रितनयः श्री वैद्यनाथसुधीः ।
भारद्वाज कुलोद्भवस्य विवुवः श्री वैडूयद्वरिह ।
ज्योतिश्शास्त्रविशारदस्य तनयः श्रीवद्यमाथः सुधीः ।।
from the abundancy of quotations found from the work
'Sarvartha Chintamani,' some opine that father of Vaidyanatha
Dikshita is none other than Venkatesa the author of 'Sarvartha
Chintamani'. But, this deserves further investigation and the
suggestion appears to be incondusive. The suggestion that the
author was indentical with the writer of the celebrated work
'Prataparadriya' on poestics, is totally incorrect, for the latter
was Vidyānāthā (विद्यानाथ), the confusion being due to the
unfortunate similarity in spelling. The appellation 'Dikshita'
clearly denotes that he must have performed some biz sacrifices
of the type 'Somayaga' etc.
The author belonged to South India and most probably
to Karnataka or Andhrapradesh.
Prof. Katva has stated that he belonged to the 14th cen-
tury and lived in Mysore. As far as we could gather, the
family tree is as follows.--
Anniah Dikshita
Venkatadri (Venkatesa Dikshita)
Vaidyanatha Dikshita
If any further details are known to he readers, they are
requested to send them to us so that the same can be added for
throwing more light about the author of this great work.
